title: neonauclea fagifolia
intro: Neonauclea fagifolia, also known as the forest kamani, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands, including Hawaii and the Philippines. It is a medicinal plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The extracts of Neonauclea fagifolia have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial in treating various health conditions such as fever, respiratory infections, and skin diseases. Herbal preparations of Neonauclea fagifolia can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or by using the extracts in capsule or tincture form, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
